mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which weakens corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP growth (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate weakening demand conditions, which create downward pressure on corporate revenues and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or 60-day forward returns in contraction phases are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity, which reduces corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading (CPIAUCSL) above 2% triggers central bank tightening expectations, which increases equity discount rates and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 100k signals strong labor market dynamics, which boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) and GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a demand-side recession, which creates severe downward pressure on equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window or 60-day forward returns in recession phases are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) following a 3-month decline signals manufacturing recovery, which improves corporate earnings outlooks and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite positive signal.
